entryPointCandidates.name: README.md
entryPointCandidates.summary: Comprehensive project guide describing the public Python API, CLI, optional HTTP service, DOCX/XLSX/PPTX authoring rules, examples, development workflow, dependencies, publishing process, and architectural constraints.

entryPointCandidates.name: CHANGELOG.md
entryPointCandidates.summary: Chronicles Stencil releases from 0.1.0 through 0.5.0, covering the DOCX foundation, PDF conversion, XLSX and PPTX engines, publishing safeguards, and the optional FastAPI service.

entryPointCandidates.name: __init__.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Defines the Stencil package's public API by re-exporting rendering, PDF-conversion, error, and version symbols.

entryPointCandidates.name: test_api.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Tests the optional FastAPI adapter's rendering response, metrics accounting, template-root confinement, and render-error reporting.

entryPointCandidates.name: create_pptx_status_template.py
entryPointCandidates.summary: Builds the styled PPTX status-deck fixture, including marker slides that demonstrate repeated-slide Jinja loops.

documentation.name: CHANGELOG.md
documentation.summary: Chronicles Stencil releases from 0.1.0 through 0.5.0, covering the DOCX foundation, PDF conversion, XLSX and PPTX engines, publishing safeguards, and the optional FastAPI service.

documentation.name: README.md
documentation.summary: Comprehensive project guide describing the public Python API, CLI, optional HTTP service, DOCX/XLSX/PPTX authoring rules, examples, development workflow, dependencies, publishing process, and architectural constraints.

documentation.name: README.md
documentation.summary: Explains the bundled DOCX and PPTX templates, their JSON payloads, authoring rules, and CLI and Python rendering workflows.

documentation.name: python-package-cicd-agent-guide.md
documentation.summary: Provides an operational playbook for reliable Python package CI/CD, covering single-source version metadata, changelog-backed releases, pull-request checks, idempotent GitHub Releases, PyPI publishing, Dependabot, branch protection, and release failure recovery. It includes ready-to-adapt TOML, YAML, Python, and shell examples plus a final checklist for future automation agents.

infrastructure.name: publish.yml
infrastructure.summary: GitHub Actions release pipeline that validates the Python package, builds distributions, derives release notes from the changelog, creates or updates a GitHub release, and publishes artifacts to PyPI on pushes to main or manual dispatch.

config.name: pyproject.toml
config.summary: Defines the office-stencil Python 3.12 package, its runtime and optional dependencies, the stencil CLI entry point, Hatchling build settings, and uv, Ruff, pytest, and strict mypy configuration.

config.name: invoice.json
config.summary: Provides a complete sample invoice payload for DOCX rendering, including invoice metadata, customer details, repeatable line items, and a formatted total.

config.name: pptx-status.json
config.summary: Supplies project metadata and milestone records used to demonstrate placeholder replacement and repeated-slide generation in a PPTX status deck.

config.name: status-report.json
config.summary: Defines the sample project status-report payload used by the styled DOCX example, with nested project and owner values, milestone rows, and an optional risk note.

config.name: dependabot.yml
config.summary: Configures weekly Dependabot updates for the project's uv and GitHub Actions dependencies, grouping Python production and development packages.

config.name: config.json
config.summary: Configures Understand-Anything to generate the project's knowledge-graph summaries, descriptions, tags, and learning content in English.

list.name: Public Interfaces
list.description: Package exports, the format-dispatch API, the Typer CLI, and the optional FastAPI adapter expose Stencil's rendering capabilities to callers.

list.name: Rendering Engine
list.description: Format-specific engines render DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX templates and convert rendered Office documents to PDF.

list.name: Package Support
list.description: Shared exception definitions, installed-version discovery, and PEP 561 typing metadata support the package's public and rendering modules.

list.name: Examples and Fixtures
list.description: Sample payloads, template fixtures, generator scripts, and walkthrough documentation demonstrate reproducible DOCX and PPTX rendering workflows.

list.name: Test Layer
list.description: Pytest integration coverage verifies the HTTP adapter and the full DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF rendering behavior.

list.name: Project Configuration and Automation
list.description: Packaging metadata, release automation, dependency updates, and Understand Anything settings govern how office-stencil is built, published, maintained, and analyzed.

list.name: Documentation
list.description: The project overview, release history, and Python package CI/CD guide explain usage, evolution, and maintenance practices.

file:src/stencil/api.py.name: api.py
file:src/stencil/api.py.summary: Optional FastAPI service exposing health, metrics, and Office-template rendering endpoints for trusted internal callers, with bounded template-path resolution and in-process render telemetry.

file:src/stencil/cli.py.name: cli.py
file:src/stencil/cli.py.summary: Typer command-line interface that loads JSON render data, dispatches Office template rendering, and writes the resulting document with user-friendly errors.

file:src/stencil/docx.py.name: docx.py
file:src/stencil/docx.py.summary: DOCX renderer that validates a template path, delegates Jinja-compatible WordprocessingML rendering to docxtpl, and returns the serialized document bytes.

file:src/stencil/errors.py.name: errors.py
file:src/stencil/errors.py.summary: Defines the Stencil exception hierarchy, including unsupported-format errors and stage-aware render failures that retain template context.

file:src/stencil/pptx.py.name: pptx.py
file:src/stencil/pptx.py.summary: PPTX rendering engine that edits Open Packaging Convention parts directly, expands slide-level Jinja loops, renders text across XML runs, and rebuilds presentation relationships and content types.

file:src/stencil/render.py.name: render.py
file:src/stencil/render.py.summary: Public format dispatcher that validates template/output combinations, routes DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX work to their renderers, and optionally converts results to PDF.

file:src/stencil/xlsx.py.name: xlsx.py
file:src/stencil/xlsx.py.summary: XLSX rendering engine that evaluates Jinja expressions in cells, expands row-loop blocks while preserving formatting, and translates copied formulas before serialization.

file:src/stencil/__init__.py.name: __init__.py
file:src/stencil/__init__.py.summary: Defines the Stencil package's public API by re-exporting rendering, PDF-conversion, error, and version symbols.

file:src/stencil/_version.py.name: _version.py
file:src/stencil/_version.py.summary: Resolves the installed office-stencil package version from importlib metadata and provides a development fallback when metadata is unavailable.

file:src/stencil/pdf.py.name: pdf.py
file:src/stencil/pdf.py.summary: Provides a PDF conversion abstraction and a bounded LibreOffice subprocess implementation for rendered DOCX, XLSX, and PPTX bytes, including retries and detailed failure handling.

pipeline:.github/workflows/publish.yml.name: publish.yml
pipeline:.github/workflows/publish.yml.summary: GitHub Actions release pipeline that validates the Python package, builds distributions, derives release notes from the changelog, creates or updates a GitHub release, and publishes artifacts to PyPI on pushes to main or manual dispatch.

document:CHANGELOG.md.name: CHANGELOG.md
document:CHANGELOG.md.summary: Chronicles Stencil releases from 0.1.0 through 0.5.0, covering the DOCX foundation, PDF conversion, XLSX and PPTX engines, publishing safeguards, and the optional FastAPI service.

config:pyproject.toml.summary: Defines the office-stencil Python 3.12 package, its runtime and optional dependencies, the stencil CLI entry point, Hatchling build settings, and uv, Ruff, pytest, and strict mypy configuration.

config:examples/data/invoice.json.name: invoice.json
config:examples/data/invoice.json.summary: Provides a complete sample invoice payload for DOCX rendering, including invoice metadata, customer details, repeatable line items, and a formatted total.

config:examples/data/pptx-status.json.name: pptx-status.json
config:examples/data/pptx-status.json.summary: Supplies project metadata and milestone records used to demonstrate placeholder replacement and repeated-slide generation in a PPTX status deck.

config:examples/data/status-report.json.name: status-report.json

document:examples/README.md.name: README.md
document:examples/README.md.summary: Explains the bundled DOCX and PPTX templates, their JSON payloads, authoring rules, and CLI and Python rendering workflows.

file:examples/scripts/create_pptx_status.name: create_pptx_status_template.py
file:examples/scripts/create_pptx_status.summary: Builds the styled PPTX status-deck fixture, including marker slides that demonstrate repeated-slide Jinja loops.

file:examples/scripts/create_styled_stat.name: create_styled_status_template.py
file:examples/scripts/create_styled_stat.summary: Generates the styled DOCX status-report fixture with headers, tables, section rules, placeholders, and conditional risk content.

file:examples/scripts/render_styled_stat.name: render_styled_status_pdf.py
file:examples/scripts/render_styled_stat.summary: Demonstrates the Python API by loading status data, rendering the styled DOCX template to PDF, and writing the result.

file:examples/templates/invoice.docx.name: invoice.docx
file:examples/templates/invoice.docx.summary: Provides the invoice DOCX fixture with Jinja variables, conditional notes, and a loop over line items.

file:examples/templates/pptx-status.pptx.name: pptx-status.pptx
file:examples/templates/pptx-status.pptx.summary: Provides the PPTX status-deck fixture with project placeholders and marker slides for cloning milestone slides.

file:examples/templates/styled-status-re.name: styled-status-report.docx
file:examples/templates/styled-status-re.summary: Provides a styled DOCX status-report fixture whose headers, tables, colors, and conditional content exercise formatting preservation.

file:src/stencil/py.typed.name: py.typed
file:src/stencil/py.typed.summary: PEP 561 marker declaring that the distributed stencil package includes inline type information.

file:tests/.gitkeep.name: .gitkeep
file:tests/.gitkeep.summary: Keeps the tests directory tracked when no other test files are present in a minimal checkout.

file:tests/test_api.py.name: test_api.py
file:tests/test_api.py.summary: Tests the optional FastAPI adapter's rendering response, metrics accounting, template-root confinement, and render-error reporting.

file:tests/test_render.py.name: test_render.py
file:tests/test_render.py.summary: Comprehensive integration suite for DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, and PDF rendering, including loops, style preservation, concurrency, and LibreOffice retry behavior.

file:.ua/.understandignore.name: .understandignore
file:.ua/.understandignore.summary: Defines the Understand-Anything analysis exclusion policy and records commented starter patterns derived from the Python-oriented repository ignore rules. It currently keeps the suggested directories, generated artifacts, caches, and test patterns available for selective activation without excluding them by default.

config:.ua/config.json.name: config.json
config:.ua/config.json.summary: Configures Understand-Anything to generate the project's knowledge-graph summaries, descriptions, tags, and learning content in English.

It includes ready-to-adapt TOML, YAML, Python, and shell examples plus a final checklist for future automation agents.
